post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 270
UIC Invitational VIII


For thousands of years, unseen angels guided humanity through some of their worst crises, artificial or otherwise, attempting to help them into a brighter future. Two centuries ago, however, the angelic community was crippled as one of the biggest companies in the morality consultation business succumbed to demonic sabotage and fell. (Like, literally, from the sky.) In the years since, they scattered to the four corners of the earth as financial mismanagement in both the human and angelic worlds created a devastating recession.

Yet even in these trying and unprecedented times, some still dreamed of a return to the golden age of angelic capitalism. Quietly, they began to search for the hidden vaults where bygone corporations had stowed billions, maybe even trillions of dollars, all of them lost to time...

- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules for easy reference.

For experienced players: everything you need is here, and in the setup tab.
For new players: you may find it useful to familiarize yourself with the rest of the rules.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside this thread, your role PM, or approved private chats, even if you are dead. Access to spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each "Day" is 10 days while each "Night" is 2 days.
  • There are no minimum deadlines.
  • Do not post during night.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to first PM prod, 72 hours to second PM prod, and 96 hours to replacement status.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization does not matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Both bold vote and code vote formats are accepted, although bold is preferred. Refer to the Voting tab if you do not know what these are.
  • Majority hang is always on but cannot end day before the minimum deadline has elapsed.
  • Deadline hang is always on except for LYLO.
  • Votes are locked at LYLO.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • Disagreements should be resolved in a way that does not affect gameplay.
  • Do not discuss rule violations by other players in the thread; contact the moderators privately.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • Players may be replaced at the moderators' discretion for procedural difficulties.
  • All other rules are standard.

Setup and Moderators
  • This game is based on the JOAT10 Setup.
  • Mafia have access to daychat.
  • All role mechanics and Night Action Resolutions are standard.
  • This game is moderated by lovematch13 and mathjay.
  • This game operates optional discord spectator and mafia private chats using the Unpaid Intern Central server. Contact the mods to request access.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contact us through your role PM or through Discord (@butlikeactually).
  • For a list of basic resources, refer to the Resources tab below.
  • For a more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, go here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not post during the night phase.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate; access to spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as appropriate.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format to be counted: Vote: [player username].
    • By default, votes should be made in [b] (bold) tags. However, votes made in [code] and [c] tags will also be accepted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is similarly formatted, even with separate tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • Vote to hang no one by posting Vote: No Hang.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Votecounts will be posted periodically. Players may request a new VC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • Votes will be carried over if a player is replaced.
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts another part of the votecount (VC).
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect VC will become official.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes at the EOD deadline, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, no one will be hanged.
    • Starting Day 1, if a player is voted by the majority of living players after the minimum deadline has elapsed, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
    • Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
    • Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each day phase will be 240 hours (10 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days).
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1).
    • If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance.
    • If a player with an NA has not submitted their action by 24 hours before the night deadline, a reminder will be sent.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 96 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM.
    • Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators.
    • Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves.
    • Do not spam.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
      Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, click here.

  • - Moderation Policies -
  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • lovematch13 and mathjay are the moderators of Mafia Game 270.
    • Other Unpaid Interns have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and management of the private chat discord system.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Purple text is used for third-party flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 (#2be368) and Activity Check blue (#005dff). For a full list of custom colors, click here.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG266 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) 10.
    Current version: 10.8.5 (Minimum Deadlines Standard Non-newbie)
    Version History

    OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, click here.
Setup
This game is based on the JOAT10 setup. This is an open setup.

In this setup, there are 10 players. The roles are as follows:

1 Town Jack of All Trades, 7 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons

The Town JOAT can choose from the following abilities:
  • 1-shot Cop
  • 1-shot Doctor
  • 1-shot Vig

Other important setup mechanics:
  • Cop results are sane.
  • The Doctor ability cannot self-target.
  • Mafia have daychat.
  • Mafia factional kills (NKs) are not compulsive.
  • Mentors may be assigned to any Town PRs as well as the Mafia faction. Do not talk about mentors in the thread.
Role PMs
Vanilla Townie Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are an Currently Unpaid, Will Continue to be Unpaid Angel (Vanilla Townie).

As a blue-linen, working class angel, this recession has hit you particularly hard. You long for the simple joys of minimum wage employment like the threadbare healthcare benefits, micromanaging managers, and that promotion that you’re definitely gonna get SoonTM. Although you proclaim your corporate enthusiasm, you still like your Jinnstagram memes with a side of anti-capitalism and your favorite sport is watching labor unions slug it out with HR on livestream. Most importantly, you know when capitalism’s gone too far.

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Click here to go to the game thread.
Town JOAT Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are the Golden Child (Town JOAT).

The absolute, perfect, quintessential unpaid intern. On time, smart, responsible, omnipresent and omni-knowledgeable, you show up in every (attempting to be an) adult’s, “Oh man, when I grow up, I want to be as good as [INSERT YOUR NAME HERE].” You’re skilled, you’re popular, you’ve achieved fun-life-work balance, and everyone knows your name— Actually, no, everyone knows Scipio1 was the original golden child, but um. So sorry. Ah, this is so awkward, but um. Which one are you again?

Each night, you may choose to use one of the following three abilities:
  • Check the alignment of a player (1-shot Cop)
  • Protect a player from the mafia factional kill (1-shot Doctor)
  • Shoot a player during the night (1-shot Vigilante)
Once you have used up all your abilities, you will no longer have access to any night actions.

You win when all Mafia are killed, so your goal is to find them. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Click here to go to the game thread.
Mafia Goon Role PM wrote:


Hello, [player]!

You are a Based Capitalism Enjoyer (Mafia Goon).



Your partner is [player] ([role]), and you may discuss with them in this private forum while you are alive. The two of you will be able to choose a player to kill each night, as well as decide who makes the kill.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this, so your goal is to stay hidden and kill Town. Play towards your win condition, and good luck.

Click here to go to the game thread.

[*]Starting Day 1, if a player is voted by the majority of living players after the minimum deadline has elapsed, then that player will be hanged immediately; and if No Hang is voted by half of living players, a No Hang will occur.
[*]Upon reaching LYLO (Hang-or-Lose), votes will be locked, meaning that they cannot be changed.
[*]Deadline hang is off at LYLO. If a majority hang is not reached by the deadline, the day will end without a hang, resulting in an automatic win for the mafia team. 
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each day phase will be 240 hours (10 days), and each night phase will be 48 hours (2 days). 
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]The current deadline can be found in the bottom of the OP (Original Post, or post #1). 
[*]If a player with an NA (Night Action) anticipates being unavailable during the night phase, they may submit any night actions to be performed in advance. 
[*]If a player with an NA has not submitted their action by 24 hours before the night deadline, a reminder will be sent. 
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform an action that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night (EON). You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates if they miss a deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive their first PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive their second PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 96 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity Checks (ACs) will be posted periodically. Players may request a new AC in bold or in their role PM. 
[*]Activity will reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, or other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Do not discuss rule violations, especially violations by other players in the thread. If you wish, contact the moderators privately through your role PM or Discord. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive player-to-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably consider alignment indicative.[/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617334]click here[/url]. 


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks. You may attack a player's opinion, but do not demean the player themselves. 
[*]Do not spam.
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines and the AoPS Terms of Service.
[*]Be mature and use common sense.
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
[b]Causing significant disruptions to gameplay may result in a force replacement at the moderators' discretion. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. A player may receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly address most etiquette issues. For specific details and examples of improper behavior, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617342]click here[/url].[/hide]

[*][b][url=https://aops.com/community/p24617357]- Moderation Policies -[/url][/b]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]lovematch13 and mathjay are the moderators of Mafia Game 270. 
[*]Other Unpaid Interns have contributed to flavor, setup, OCRSTM, feedback, and management of the private chat discord system.
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access as appropriate.
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators. 
[*][color=#00FF00]Green text[/color] is used for town flavor-related text.
[*][color=#9a00ff]Purple text[/color] is used for third-party flavor-related text.
[*]Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes. 
[*]Current custom shades are: Town flavor green v.2 ([b][color=#2be368]#2be368[/color][/b]) and Activity Check blue ([b][color=#005dff]#005dff[/color][/b]). For a full list of custom colors, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24617373]click here[/url]. 
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in the OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.[/list]

[b]MG266 OP is based on Overcomplicated Ruleset[sup]TM[/sup] (OCRSTM) 10.[/b]
[i]Current version: 10.8.5 (Minimum Deadlines Standard Non-newbie)[/i]
[url=https://aops.com/community/p21335350]Version History[/url]

[b]OCRSTM 10 is available for public use. If you are interested in using OCRSTM in your own games, [url=https://aops.com/community/p24637777]click here[/url]. [/b]
[/hide][/list]
[hide=Setup]This game is based on the [url=https://www.mafiauniverse.com/wiki/JOAT10er]JOAT10[/url] setup. This is an open setup. 

In this setup, there are 10 players. The roles are as follows: 

[b]1 Town Jack of All Trades, 7 Vanilla Townies, 2 Mafia Goons[/b]

The Town JOAT can choose from the following abilities: 
[list][*]1-shot Cop
[*]1-shot Doctor
[*]1-shot Vig[/list]

Other important setup mechanics: 
[list][*]Cop results are sane. 
[*]The Doctor ability cannot self-target. 
[*]Mafia have daychat.
[*]Mafia factional kills (NKs) are not compulsive. 
[*]Mentors may be assigned to any Town PRs as well as the Mafia faction. Do not talk about mentors in the thread. [/list]

Although you proclaim your corporate enthusiasm, you still like your Jinnstagram memes with a side of anti-capitalism and your favorite sport is watching labor unions slug it out with HR on livestream. Most importantly, you know when capitalism’s gone too far. [/i]

Your power lies in your observation skills, your voice, and your vote.

post_rendered:
200 years, 10 months, and 20 days since the attempted terrorist hijacking of a milk factory owned by the Incredibly Capitalism beverage corporation...

We’re here, said ad333.

Everyone peered over his shoulder at a nondescript wooden door with a sign on it that said Do Not Enter.

Msv, where are we? HannahW asked.
Mathletesv tapped his wrist and squinted at the tiny projection. We’re in Oxford.
We’re in England? she said, surprised.
No, we’re in Ohio.
What, said HannahW.
Mathletesv sighed. We’re in the city of Oxford, Ohio, on the campus of what used to be Miami University. They went bankrupt a long time ago. I believe this exact building was called the... He looked at his wrist again and wrinkled his nose. The Francis Gibson [BLEEP] Hall?

HannahW blinked.

Did you just get... censored? she asked in disbelief.
Mathletesv just raised his hands and shrugged helplessly.
._. said HannahW. Msv, I am very disapp—

She was interrupted as Socoobo swaggered over and whacked an arm over her shoulder. Ayo, Hannah, chill chill chill. It's all good! We're all here to get rich, yea?

YEA!! cheered hellomynameisjoe, before quickly correcting herself. I mean, uhm, that's not the exact reason we're here, but it's probably gonna be a side effect, so... :thumbs_up:
Why are we here? asked ++aroodman, crossing his arms.

I can answer that, said falliinqsnow-. stepping forward. We started digging around for the financial papers of the old corporations we worked at because we um. Well. We keep running out of money.

Scb died rich as frick and he still acts like he's loaded, Mathletesv grumbled.

MSV UR FIREDTM yelled Socoobo from the floor where he was playing Dota 2: The Second Sequel with his god-forsaken keybinds.
HannahW blinked. How did scb teleport from me to dota in 2 seconds.

falliinqsnow- coughed. Anyways. It's been years since everything went out of business. We kinda got tired of not being paid and thought, hey, maybe some money got stuck in the seals of the money laundry machine! And you know, we were bored and it was a nice way to pass time, but then ad actually— wait, where's ad??

Don't worry, he'll show up eventually, Mathletesv said absently, already examining the door.

Okayyy... said falliinqsnow-. He found something. Um. This.
She waved vaguely at the door.
And now we might actually get paid a decent wage! cheered hellomynameisjoe.

From a storage closet, ++chess24 said skeptically.

But with money inside! hellomynameisjoe reminded him at the same time as Mathletesv argued, You don't know that it's a storage closet.

An alleged storage closet with money, ++chess24 corrected.
Whose money is it? asked HannahW.

Yours, replied ad333, appearing out of nowhere. Everyone jumped except for Mathletesv, who just rolled his eyes.

I do not remember putting money in a storage closet, said HannahW. Nor does that sound very advisable.

It's not a storage closet! protested Mathletesv. It's a vault! Of some kind. Extremely well protected. Incredibly well protected, at least on the paper side. Ad, how many shell companies did you say there were? he asked.

270— said ad333.
That’s not too bad, ++fdas remarked.
—thousand.

Ah... HannahW said. I don't think I remember that either.
It's ok, said ad333. We might find some answers inside.
smh foreshadowing, grumbled Mathletesv. It does say Do Not Enter on the door.
Yes, we should follow the rules, agreed ++fdas.

NOT IF U WANNA GET RICH, hollered Socoobo as he threw open the door and stepped through. WHOO— AHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH...

Everyone watched as he fell down into the darkness.

Patching last minute plot holes, to be continued...

Hannah feels like she's genuinely feeling the game out and exploring on her own without making a show of it, and town messiness and all.

ad and chess are the next tier. ad just... occasionally makes me look back at a point he made and go "oh wait, yeah true" in a way I find towny, although I'd be lying if I said I'm not still skeeved by him a good amount. chess's approach here is pretty much exactly what I would've expected from v!him here just from my memory of his play, and his gamestate awareness (oh look, a read I'm sponging from ad) feels towny, I just wouldn't put it past him to play like this as a wolf.

That leaves fdas, joe, Msv and snow.

fdas is utterly and completely fdas.

I did want to accept ad's/Wisteria's point about joe, that it's hard to make original reads when everything's already talked about and consensus, as a good enough explanation for joe being this underwhelming, but I'm not quite there, in no small part because she doesn't seem to have really talked much about the consensus, and with reads like this,

I'm thinking that the JOAT does out if they have a peek

They can use the vig tonight guaranteed, and since we'd be in odd numbers after that, the doc is basically useless; it can't get us more misses, it can only clear whoever it saves, and wolves aren't gonna shoot someone who isn't mech clear or obvious town anyway.

Plus, with a default PoE of ad/fdas/joe/Msv/snow, if we get a clear in there, we technically have a winning PoE if everything else is right but we'd have to check everything. If we get two clears in there because one of them is the JOAT, we're sitting pretty comfy, and we might just outright catch a wolf today from not-w/w reads in such a narrow PoE.

Open to objections. I also realize I'm hard spewing myself as VT, but I figured the fact that it's me, in an AoPS game, and there was no vig shot N1, would've already made that obvious to wolves.

fdas wrote:
Joat shouldn't claim here, ideally we'd keep the conftowns under wraps for as long as possible. If the JOAT got a redcheck they can just shoot them tonight. If they got a greencheck, we do the thing where everyone drops a fake peek at the end of today. The exception is if we're about to hang whoever they greenchecked. If Joat claims early they get shot and we go into lylo without any conf towns. If they claim the day before lylo we'd have our 2 greenchecks whittled down to 1 when lylo starts and if they claim during lylo we either have 2 conf towns if there is no CC or we put it up to a 50/50. Both of these scenarios are better than not having any mech clears in lylo.

1. If the JOAT misses tonight, this is the day before LYLO.

2. It's not like wolves don't have plenty of virtual clears to kill already, even if they don't have any mechanical clears yet, so it really makes very little difference how many are out there for them to kill.

3. There are a number of pretty hard not w/w pairs in PoE. If one's a redchecked wolf, we don't wanna wait for the JOAT to shoot them lol, we wanna know if someone's clear off of it right now. Plus there's often just a lot of spew you can get out of any wolf anyway.

4. It's been shown time and time again that having extra clears is just good for the gamestate lol, like that's what the whole bucket strat in champs S8 was about. I think it'd be different if it was a cop instead of a JOAT and they could still pull in more peeks, but if it's just "we want innocent children to stay hidden"? Nah.

JOAT cover theoretically works, but like, do wolves actually have significant reason to hunt the hidden JOAT with everything they've got over just killing someone who's obvious town and clearly never getting MLed? I'd much, much rather just go "The JOAT survived the only night that mattered and we should cash in" than hope for the sliver of a chance that happens.

.

post_rendered:
falliinqsnow- wrote:
hey arood, i really want to compare reads with you right now. can you give thoughts on ad/msv/hannah both singularly and as a dynamic?

ad

What gives me the most doubt on ad being a wolf is that he has this occasional outlandishness and disconnectedness that makes him seem like he's in his own world and unbothered by everyone else. Socoobo put it better, some snippet I quoted during EoD1 that I forget the exact wording of and don't feel like scrolling up to rn.

The thing is, that's... the only towny thing I've ever seen ad do as a wolf in the past. Like, that was basically his entire strategy for getting townread in 251.

The rest of his play is rough, to put it nicely. Everything that I could've said about his EoD has been said plenty by everyone else, his focus is terrible, and a lot of reads just don't really strike me as genuine. When I said "Is ad trying to be wolfy", it was about his Wisteria push ofc, but not because of his actual arguments. More because that was all he felt like commenting on, after all that time, when Wist is as blatantly town as she is otherwise. It read more like "I had a thought that I can turn into a push" than "I had a thought that I should turn into a push". It doesn't help that inauthentic reads and bad focus are like, his signature wolftells.

Msv

His reaction to chess dying felt pure. I feel like he believes his Hannah push. He's generally playing like how I remember Msv playing as town.

Can he be a wolf? Absolutely.

Just not an immediate concern, though, and he's unaligned from other members of (the relatively small) PoE, which helps. I don't feel like reading the tea leaves that are his posts to nail down his alignment further.

Hannah

The short version is that the way she's acting is iffy, but the way she's thinking is very towny. I've already written about it, Wisteria's already written about it (and we basically wrote the same thing), and beyond just the broad "wide focus/towny messiness" point there are a handful of micro moments I liked that I might dig up if people still aren't sold on her. Whenever I go back to read all of her play at once, she just feels... obvious, and while I do get doubt on her when I'm thinking about the game big picture and realizing that she hasn't been all that impactful on the gamestate, I'm not enthused by the thought of "I'm gonna abandon my townread on Hannah because I doubt it when I'm not actually reading her posts".

Imma make one last effort on the JOAT thing.
fdas wrote:
aroodman wrote:
Does anyone have a reason the JOAT shouldn't out? Because every way I look at it, it seems dumb not for them to out, although there's an argument for something like "they should wait until halfway through the day".

If JOAT is someone who is currently in the towncore and have a greencheck then they shouldn't claim. They can go into LYLO and win the claim war against scum. So if scum CCs the scum gets hanged and the game is basically free. If the scum doesn't CC we have 2 mech clears out of 5 so we only need to find the 1 towny person out of the remaining 3 to win. If JOAT is currently seen as scummy then they can claim.

If JOAT is in the towncore and have a redcheck, from a mechanical standpoint not claiming and vigging the mafia and then hoping to survive until lylo to be the 1 clear out of 3 is better than claiming, hanging the mafia, and then getting night killed, leaving us with no clears in lylo. But your argument about improving the gamestate does make some sense, but I'm not sure how I want to reconcile that consideration with concrete mechanical benefits of not claiming.

The thing about the JOAT not needing to claim if they're universally townread does make sense on its own, they can win the claim battle most of the time. It's not guaranteed since everything gets reevaluated at LYLO, but if it's someone like me (even though it isn't lol) or Wisteria, the JOAT almost always wins...

...but I feel like at that point, if your premise is "there are people here who are clearly never getting MLed", which there are, then you have to acknowledge that they're more or less as useful to us as an actual mechanical clear. From wolves' PoV, there isn't a lot of difference if any between killing a literal innocent child and killing an effective innocent child, so if there's even one effective innocent child, any benefit of trying to hide the JOAT until LYLO goes down the drain. If the JOAT used their check last night, they are functionally only an innocent child from a mech standpoint, with the only difference being that they can make someone else an innocent child too by claiming, or give us a player's worth of spew to read today by claiming. Literally the only way the JOAT can be more useful than they already are is by claiming, if you agree that there are literally any social clears, which, come on, there clearly are.

People are already spending a good amount of their energy today building teams. One clear makes that process a lot easier when there are only 8 alive. Two clears is even better. It seems really, really bizarre for the JOAT not to claim today if they have a peek of any kind. I don't think it's complicated.

If JOAT is someone who is currently in the towncore and have a greencheck then they shouldn't claim. They can go into LYLO and win the claim war against scum. So if scum CCs the scum gets hanged and the game is basically free. If the scum doesn't CC we have 2 mech clears out of 5 so we only need to find the 1 towny person out of the remaining 3 to win. If JOAT is currently seen as scummy then they can claim.

If JOAT is in the towncore and have a redcheck, from a mechanical standpoint not claiming and vigging the mafia and then hoping to survive until lylo to be the 1 clear out of 3 is better than claiming, hanging the mafia, and then getting night killed, leaving us with no clears in lylo. But your argument about improving the gamestate does make some sense, but I'm not sure how I want to reconcile that consideration with concrete mechanical benefits of not claiming.[/quote]

The thing about the JOAT not needing to claim if they're universally townread does make sense on its own, they can win the claim battle most of the time. It's not guaranteed since everything gets reevaluated at LYLO, but if it's someone like me (even though it isn't lol) or Wisteria, the JOAT almost always wins...

...but I feel like at that point, if your premise is "there are people here who are clearly never getting MLed", which there are, then you have to acknowledge that they're more or less as useful to us as an actual mechanical clear. From wolves' PoV, there isn't a lot of difference if any between killing a literal innocent child and killing an effective innocent child, so if there's even one effective innocent child, any benefit of trying to hide the JOAT until LYLO goes down the drain. If the JOAT used their check last night, they are functionally [i]only[/i] an innocent child from a mech standpoint, with the only difference being that they can make someone else an innocent child too by claiming, or give us a player's worth of spew to read today by claiming. Literally the only way the JOAT can be more useful than they already are is by claiming, if you agree that there are [i]literally any[/i] social clears, which, come on, there clearly are.

People are already spending a good amount of their energy today building teams. One clear makes that process a lot easier when there are only 8 alive. Two clears is even better. It seems really, really bizarre for the JOAT not to claim today if they have a peek of any kind. I don't think it's complicated.[/quote]

I'll concede the point.

Msv feels like a complete chaos factor. Nothing they do makes sense and if they were mafia they’d likely just be trying to muddle the waters so much we end up going in circles trying to analyze him. post_rendered:
Btw Hannah I thought your day play was great, but using the doc N1 in this setup is pretty much 100% wrong

We do start on even numbers, so it's true that getting a save lets us resolve one extra slot by giving us an extra miss. The thing is... both of the other abilities let us resolve one extra slot too. The vig also puts us in odds, with a much greater guarantee than the doc, and they can't both give us that +1 KP because that would just be putting us back in evens. The cop resolves an extra slot by, well, being a cop.

Honestly the doc is kinda useless in this setup most of the time, and the only utility it has at all over the other abilities is that it clears whoever it saves, but I mean... clearing someone who was positioned such that wolves wanted to kill them is usually pretty useless too lol.

Like, if you got the fdas redcheck N1 instead of N2, you would've cleared ad too, and we'd basically win on autopilot.

post_rendered:
I think that exercise is especially useful for a misread like this, because it's a kind of misread you could keep doing again and again in different contexts because it looks different every time

Like, you can obviously say "Next game I won't townread someone for tonal differences from her last and only wolf game where she got caught D1 when she hasn't done anything towny-in-a-vacuum all game", but like, you can use the exercise to start thinking about the difference between good reads like that and bad reads like that

Because unless you're a player like Apoc or something, you're obviously not gonna stop saying "This player is town for being different from their wolf meta" altogether lol, nor should you

So it's like, food for thought about what kinds of things can more easily change from wolf game to wolf game, and you can start thinking of more generalized approaches
